project.name: SylphxAI Atoma
project.summary: TypeScript state-management library experiment centered on a framework-agnostic atom() and store() API.
goals: Provide a minimal framework-agnostic state management core with atoms, stores, computed state, async state, streams, models, and families.
Keep the public package surface focused on src/index.ts, src/atom.ts, src/store.ts, and src/types.ts.
Stabilize the family API and TypeScript inference before production lifecycle promotion.
nonGoals: Own application-specific state models or UI framework integrations until they are explicitly added.
Own downstream product behavior that consumes the package.
Own enterprise engineering doctrine, standards, or project manifest schema.

owns.name: atoma-core-package
owns.description: The framework-agnostic state management package source, public exports, package manifest, and tests.

owns.name: atoma-state-api
owns.description: The atom(), store(), computed, async, stream, model, and family state semantics documented in README.md and implemented under src.

doesNotOwn: Application-specific state models, UI framework adapters, or downstream product behavior.
Package registry administration or organization-wide package release policy.
Enterprise doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine

forbiddenCouplings: Do not add product-specific state models for a downstream application to this shared package.
Do not add framework-specific adapters without a documented package contract and boundary.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: Main-branch Release workflow delegates to SylphxAI/.github reusable release workflow, but production promotion is blocked by recorded TypeScript/API instability.
delivery.productionProof: npm run build, npm test, typecheck once added/enforced, successful release workflow, and package-registry readback for published versions.

gaps.description: memory-bank/progress.md records widespread TypeScript errors from an incomplete atom family API refactor.
The repository has a main-branch release workflow, but no PR validation workflow or required context is recorded for build/test/typecheck.
gaps.target: central ADR-29 required-context rollout or repo-local package validation gate

gaps.description: package.json repository and issue URLs still point at github.com/sylphlab/atoma rather than SylphxAI/atoma.
